rulings.Case Title: MAIN STREET BUSINESS LOANS, LLC VS. OVERFLOW TRUCKING, INC. A GEORGIA CORPORTION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition And Motion To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 15, 2015, Line 1, PETITIONER MAIN STREET BUSINESS LOANS, LLC'S Hearing On Petition And Motion To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award. Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)
rulings.Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith

rulings.Case Title: WHEELER SUPPORTIVE SERVICES, INC. VS. GOLDEN GATE REGIONAL CENTER, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing For Issuance Of Peremptory Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 15, 2015, Line 3, PETITIONER WHEELER SUPPORTIVE SERVICES, INC.'S Hearing For Issuance Of Peremptory Writ Of Mandate. Continued to September 23, 2015 to give Plaintiff Wheeler Supportive Services, Inc. the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Plaintiff Wheeler Supportive Services, Inc. must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than September 15, 2015 at 4:00 p.m. or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the [moving/opposition/reply] papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EG)
rulings.Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith

rulings.Case Title: RAIN O DAUGHERTY VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Records That Were Lodged Provisionally Under Seal By Respondents On May 29, 2015 And June 5, 2015
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 15, 2015, Line 10, Motion To Seal Records That Were Lodged Provisionally Under Seal By Respondents On May 29, 2015 And June 5, 2015. Granted. All references to material protected by Penal Code sections 832.7 and 832.8 must be filed under seal pursuant to the procedures set forth in Rule 2.550 and 2.551. Copley Press, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (2006) 39 Cal.4th 1272, 1296 establishes that the Legislature did not intend for an officer to have to choose between protecting his rights via an action under the Peace Officers Bill of Rights and his right of confidentiality as set forth in the Penal Code. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)
rulings.Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith

rulings.Case Title: ABDUR SALIH VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Deposition And Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, September 15, 2015, Line 1, Defendant City And County Of San Francisco's Notice of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Deposition And Proof Of Service. Pro Tem Judge Steven B. Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's Motion to Compel the deposition of the plaintiff is granted without opposition. The deposition shall be held on or before October 30, 2015. The Court notes plaintiff's counsel on October 15, 2015, in Dept. 302, has scheduled a motion for permission to withdraw as counsel based on inability locate his client for the past year. Plaintiff's counsel shall make all appropriate attempts to notify to plaintiff as to the issuance of this Order. If unsuccessful counsel shall submit a further declaration in support of his withdrawal motion reflecting the efforts to locate his client. The moving party to prepare the Order setting forth the ruling herein. Any party who contests this tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org and sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm the court day prior to the hearing stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem Judge hearing the motion and the Pro Tem Judge will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare an order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Steven B. Stein

rulings.Case Title: PAUL GALINDO et al VS. SECURITY TITLE INSURANCE CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Paula Buckley, John Adams, Stephen Abrush, Linell Brandon And Tony Aumavae'S Motion For Protective Order Denying Defendants Civic Center Hotel, Llc And Balwantsinh Thakors' Demand For Inspection Of Units
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, September 15, 2015, Line 3, Plaintiffs Paula Buckley, John Adams, Stephen Abrush, Linell Brandon And Tony Aumavae's Motion For Protective Order Denying Defendants Civic Center Hotel, LLC And Balwantsinh Thakors' Demand For Inspection Of Units. Pro Tem Judge Steven B. Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's Motion for a Protective Order is granted. Although belatedly served, the plaintiffs formally their premises inspection to take place at the same time as set by the defendant and had advised the defendant of its request to do so in furtherance of the "meet and confer" process. The Court in its discretion grants plaintiffs' CCP 2031.300(a) application to permit late service of their formal response and has considered the objections on the merits. Plaintiff's objections as to relevance and invasion of privacy in connection with the defendant's inspection are overruled. However with no trial date presently set, a short delay in working out the details of a joint inspection makes sense with obvious savings to both sides' litigation costs and time. If as a result of late service of plaintiffs' objection the defendant suffered an out-of-pocket loss in the nature of expert fees it was required to pay due to the late cancellation of the inspection, plaintiffs shall reimburse the defendant for same. The Order to be entered shall include details as to how the parties' will ensure protection of the plaintiffs' rights of privacy. The plaintiffs' request for sanctions is denied. Plaintiff's motion to compel the deposition of defendants PMK is granted. The deposition shall be scheduled to commence on or prior to September 30, 201 and shall continue day to day until completed unless counsel shall otherwise agree. The Court will hear oral argument and rule on the categories of PMK testimony which remain in dispute. Any party who contests this tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org and sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm the court day prior to the hearing stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem Judge hearing the motion and the Pro Tem Judge will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare an order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Steven B. Stein

rulings.Case Title: JORGE VALERA VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Jorge Valera'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One (Further Hearing On Protective Order Issues)
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, September 15, 2015, Line 5, Plaintiff Jorge Valera's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's Request For Production Of Documents, Set One (Further Hearing On Protective Order Issues). Pro Tem Judge Steven B. Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The Court will finalize the Order relating to document production and rule on the language of the protective order after oral argument. The Court is inclined to include a "Highly Confidential" document category which is strictly limited to actual technical documents created by Ford engineers, distributed internally only, which sets forth the unique hardware-software calibration combinations tested and adjusted in such combinations which differ slightly based on year, make and model, and considered by Ford as secret information. Any party who contests this tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org and sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm the court day prior to the hearing stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem Judge hearing the motion and the Pro Tem Judge will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare an order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Steven B. Stein

rulings.Case Title: FREDERICK W. MCCARTHY III VS. VINTAGE PRESS LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 15, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANT STEVEN LAU'S Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default. The motion is granted. In light of the liberal policy of hearing cases on the merits, the court finds that plaintiff demonstrates excusable neglect, brought this motion within a reasonable time and provided a proposed pleading. Defendant Lau shall remit $500 to plaintiff for the costs of obtaining the default. See Rogalski v. Nabers Cadillac (1992) 11 Cal.App.4th 816, 822-823. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: SANDEEP HUNDAL VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Ford Motor Company'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Memorandum Of Points And Authorities Declaration Of H Paul Efstratis In Support Proposed Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, September 15, 2015, Line 9, Defendant Ford Motor Company's Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Memorandum Of Points And Authorities Declaration Of H Paul Efstratis In Support Proposed Protective Order. Pro Tem Judge Steven B. Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The Court will finalize the Order relating to document production and rule on the language of the protective order after oral argument. The Court is inclined to include a "Highly Confidential" document category which is strictly limited to actual technical documents created by Ford engineers, distributed internally only, which sets forth the unique hardware-software calibration combinations tested and adjusted in such combinations which differ slightly based on year, make and model, and considered by Ford as secret information. Any party who contests this tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org and sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm the court day prior to the hearing stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem Judge hearing the motion and the Pro Tem Judge will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare an order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Steven B. Stein

rulings.Case Title: PETER M OWENS VS. IRIS CANADA ET AL

rulings.Case Title: MERY PEREZ VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Hearing - Notice Of Motion And Motion By Defendant Derrick Deh-Rhong Lung, M.D. To Compel Further Responses To Discovery With Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Orin Zebest
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, September 15, 2015, Line 11, Defendant Derrick Deh-Rhong Lung, M.D. Amended Notice Of Hearing - Notice Of Motion And Motion By Defendant Derrick Deh-Rhong Lung, M.D. To Compel Further Responses To Discovery With Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Orin Zebest. Pro Tem Judge Steven B. Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Defendant Zebest has appeared by counsel who apparently served opposition to the motion but failed to file same with the Register of Actions or provide a courtesy hard copy to the Discovery Department. The Court in its discretion will review and consider the opposition at the hearing based on the fact defendant Zebest has not voluntarily put in issue his medical condition prior to the incident at SFGH. The substance of the discovery sought involves his right of privacy. Discovery of same must be evaluated and balanced based on the co-defendant's need. Further, the moving party has failed in his moving papers to demonstrate the relevancy of the discovery sought, especially in the context of the granting of the recent motion to compel discovery of the Zebest medical records from San Francisco General Hospital. The Court will consider the moving party's oral argument to supplement the written record. Any party who contests this tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org and sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm the court day prior to the hearing stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem Judge hearing the motion and the Pro Tem Judge will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare an order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Steven B. Stein

rulings.Case Title: BAF SPECIALTY, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. KEENAN HOPKINS SUDER & STOWELL CONTRACTORS INC., A ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 15, 2015, Line 8, DEFENDANT KEENAN HOPKINS SUDER & STOWELL CONTRACTORS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant Keenan Hopkin's Demurrer is OVERRULED. Taking all material facts pleaded as true, Sheehan v. San Francisco 49ers, Ltd. (2009) 45 Cal.4th 992, 998, Plaintiff states facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action. (Code Civ. Pro. § 431.10(e).) Plaintiff alleges: (1) Plaintiff and Defendant entered into a contract; (2) Defendant breached the contract by misrepresenting that the inspector rejected Plaintiff's work; and (3) Defendant has been harmed in an amount in excess of $250,000. (See Complaint 10, 25, 35.) That Defendant is withholding any payment from Plaintiff on the basis that Defendant must recoup for its own losses is sufficient to constitute a cause of action. (Code Civ. Pro. § 431.10(e).) Defendant's position that Plaintiff would not leave a work area until it passed inspection does not properly address Plaintiff's allegation that Defendant misrepresented whether Plaintiff passed inspection. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)
rulings.Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith
